pages.raw_response: {"product_name": "AURF7675M2TR", "product_type": "Automotive DirectFET Power MOSFET", "application_area": "Automotive Class D audio amplifier applications", "key_features": ["Advanced Process Technology", "Optimized for Class D Audio Amplifier Applications", "Low Rds(on) for Improved Efficiency", "Low Qg for Better THD and Improved Efficiency", "Low Qrr for Better THD and Lower EMI", "Low Parasitic Inductance for Reduced Ringing and Lower EMI", "Delivers up to 250W per Channel into 4\u03a9 with No Heat sink", "Dual Sided Cooling", "175\u00b0C Operating Temperature", "Repetitive Avalanche Capability for Robustness and Reliability", "Lead free, RoHS and Halogen free", "Automotive Qualified"], "description": "The AURF7675M2TR/TR combines the latest Automotive HEXFET Power MOSFET Silicon technology with the advanced DirectFET packaging platform to produce a best in class part for Automotive Class D audio amplifier applications. The DirectFET package is compatible with existing layout geometries used in power applications, PCB assembly equipment and vapor phase, infra-red or convection soldering techniques, when application note AN-1035 is followed regarding the manufacturing methods and processes. The DirectFET package allows dual sided cooling to maximize thermal transfer in automotive power systems. This HEXFET Power MOSFET optimizes gate charge, body diode reverse recovery and internal gate resistance to improve key Class D audio amplifier performance factors such as efficiency, THD and EMI. Moreover the DirectFET packaging platform offers low parasitic inductance and resistance when compared to conventional wire bonded SOIC packages which improves EMI performance by reducing the voltage {"product_name": "AURF7675M2TR", "notes_on_repetitive_avalanche_curves": "1. Avalanche failures assumption: Purely a thermal phenomenon and failure occurs at a temperature far in excess of Tjmax. This is validated for every part type.
2. Safe operation in Avalanche is allowed as long as Tjmax is not exceeded.
3. Equation below based on circuit and waveforms shown in Figures 18a, 18b.
4. Pd(ave) = Average power dissipation per single avalanche pulse.
5. BV = Rated breakdown voltage (1.3 factor accounts for voltage increase during avalanche).
6. Iav = Allowable avalanche current.
7. ΔT = Allowable rise in junction temperature, not to exceed Tjmax (assumed as 25°C in Figure 16, 17).
8. tav = Average time in avalanche.
9. D = Duty cycle in avalanche = tav / t.
10. ZthJC(D, tav) = Transient thermal resistance, see Figures 15)", "equation_for_power_dissipation": "Pd(ave) = 1/2 • (1.3•BV•Iav) = ΔT / ZthJC", "equation_for_allowable_avalanche_current": "Iav = 2•T / (1.3•BV•ZthJC)", "equation_for_avalanche_energy": "EAS(AR) = Pd(ave)•tav", "figure_17_description": "Maximum Avalanche Energy vs. Temperature", "figure_18a_description": "Unclamped Inductive Test Circuit", "figure_18b_description": "Unclamped Inductive Waveforms", "figure_19a_description": "Gate Charge Test Circuit", "figure_19b_description": "Gate Charge Waveform", "figure_20a_description": "Switching Time Test Circuit", "figure_20b_description": "Switching Time Waveforms", "page_number": 7, "document_date": "Dec 14, 2015", "company_logo": "infineon", "document_title": "AURF7675M2TR", "parameters": [], "figures": [{"label": "Fig 17", "y_axis": "EAS - Avalanche Energy", "y_axis_unit": "mJ", "x_axis": "Starting Tj", "x_axis_unit": "°C", "grid_position": "left"}, {"label": "Fig 18a", "y_axis": "Voltage (Vds, Vgs)", "y_axis_unit": "V", "x_axis": "Time (t)", "x_axis_unit": "s", "grid_position": "left"}, {"label": "Fig 18b", "y_axis": "Voltage (V(BR)DSS)", "y_axis_unit": "V", "x_axis": "Time (tp)", "x_axis_unit": "s", "grid_position": "right"}, {"label": "Fig 19a", "y_axis": "Voltage (Vds, Vgs)", "y_axis_unit": "V", "x_axis": "Time (t)", "x_axis_unit": "s", "grid_position": "left"}, {"label": "Fig 19b", "y_axis": "Current (Id)", "y_axis_unit": "A", "x_axis": "Voltage (Vds, Vgs)", "x_axis_unit": "V", "grid_position": "right"}, {"label": "Fig 20a", "y_axis": "Voltage (Vds, Vgs)", "y_axis_unit": "V", "x_axis": "Time (t)", "x_axis_unit": "s", "grid_position": "left"}, {"label": "Fig 20b", "y_axis": "Voltage (VDS, VGS)", "y_axis_unit": "V", "x_axis": "Time (t1(on), t2(off))", "x_axis_unit": "s", "grid_position": "right"}]}
